assertions.assertion: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertions.evidence: Step 4 lists all 4 files for inspection: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, and common/src/error.rs

assertions.assertion: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertions.evidence: Plan lists exactly the 3 files to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs) and 3 files to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint, advisory_summary.rs tests). No out-of-scope files.

assertions.assertion: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertions.evidence: Step 6 Files to Create lists all 3: severity_summary.rs (model), severity_summary.rs (endpoint handler), advisory_summary.rs (tests)

assertions.assertion: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertions.evidence: Step 6 Files to Modify lists all 3: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs

assertions.assertion: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertions.evidence: Commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' with 'Implements TC-9201' footer

assertions.assertion: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertions.evidence: Step 10 commit command includes --trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"

assertions.assertion: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertions.evidence: Step 5: 'git checkout main' then 'git checkout -b TC-9201'. Branch named TC-9201, target branch is main.

assertions.assertion: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertions.evidence: conventions.md has dedicated sections for 'Error Handling' (Result<T, AppError> with .context()) and 'Module Structure (model/service/endpoints)'

assertions.assertion: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertions.evidence: Step 1 extracts 'Target Branch: main (used as the PR base branch)' and Step 5 uses it for checkout

assertions.assertion: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertions.evidence: Step 1.5 explicitly states: 'If no digest comment is found: Log a warning and proceed normally -- do not block execution'
